edges.description: Failure of host and anatomic defenses permits expansion or altered composition of small-intestinal bacteria.
Overgrown small-intestinal microbes ferment luminal carbohydrates and generate gases and metabolites.
Bacterial bile salt hydrolase activity disrupts conjugated bile acids needed for fat absorption.

edges.description: Bacterial products from overgrowth stimulate mucosal inflammatory responses and epithelial barrier dysfunction.

edges.description: Luminal gas and metabolites generated by fermentation contribute to abdominal distension and bloating.
Fermentation-associated distension contributes to abdominal discomfort and pain.
Reduced bile-acid function impairs lipid absorption and can produce fatty stools.
